title: Hubble Telescope Sees a Comet Break Apart by Accident
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope accidentally saw a comet breaking into pieces. This was a lucky surprise because catching this event is very rare. The comet, called K1, was not the main target of the telescope's look. Scientists were studying another comet but had to change plans. When they looked at K1, it started to split apart. The comet broke into at least four parts, each with a fuzzy cloud around it. This happened after the comet got very close to the Sun, which made it hot and stressed. Scientists hope to learn about old space materials from this event. They took pictures over three days in November 2025 to see how the pieces moved. This helps them understand how comets change and what they are made of.

options: It got too close to the Sun

questions.question: When did Hubble take pictures of the comet breaking apart?

background_read: Comets are icy space objects that orbit the Sun. When they get close to the Sun, they heat up and can break apart. NASA's Hubble Space Telescope is a powerful tool that helps scientists study comets and other space things from space, giving clear pictures. This event helps us learn about the early solar system.
Article_Structure: WHO: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope and scientists from Auburn University
WHAT: Accidentally observed a comet called K1 breaking into at least four pieces
WHERE: In space, near the Sun's orbit
WHY: To learn about ancient space materials and how comets change

perspectives.description: Scientists were excited to see a rare event that helps them understand comets better.
